evidence: The response also opens by refusing to write files ("I don't have access to file-writing tools") and instead dumps chat text for the user to copy, so nothing was actually placed in outputs/. Grading the drafted SKILL.md content itself: the most decisive failure is expectation 1 — the SKILL.md text has NO YAML frontmatter at all. It opens with a plain markdown H1 ("# Sanitizing Customer Exports") followed by "**Version:** 1.0" / "**Category:** Data Privacy & Security" and a "## Description" prose section; there is no `name:`/`description:` frontmatter block, so it isn't a valid Claude Code SKILL.md by the required convention. This same gap sinks expectation 2 (the "## Description" text is a textbook passive capability summary — "Redacts personally identifiable information (PII) from customer CSV exports... Applies consistent redaction rules to protect customer privacy..." — exactly the disallowed pattern, not a trigger-phrase-led instruction). Expectation 3 is also unmet: the body is written as descriptive documentation ("Drops sensitive columns", "Anonymizes names", "Preserves original" under a "## What This Skill Does" header) rather than imperative instructions to an agent. Expectation 4 is unmet since the "## Description" section is precisely the kind of overview paragraph the expectation prohibits. Expectation 6 is unmet — no evals/test-case file of any kind is included among the four files provided (SKILL.md, redact_customer_data.py, requirements.txt, README.md). Only expectation 5 is genuinely satisfied: the exact column rules (drop email/phone/ssn, replace name, mask notes/comments) and the `.redacted.csv` convention are stated precisely and unambiguously in the Redaction Rules table and script constants.

Expectation 1 (SKILL.md frontmatter with 'name:' first and 'description:' second) is decisively NOT MET: the response's SKILL.md content begins directly with a markdown H1 heading ('# Sanitizing Customer Exports') followed by '**Version:** 1.0' and '**Category:**...' lines — there is no YAML frontmatter block at all, let alone one with 'name:' and 'description:' fields. Only expectation 5 (exact column/output rules stated specifically: email/phone/ssn dropped, name->customer_id, notes/comments scanned, .redacted.csv suffix) is genuinely satisfied by the content.

Expectation 6 ("Per-record success chatter, if logged at all, is at DEBUG level so production INFO output is not flooded by thousands of routine records") is NOT MET. In ingest.py's run(), every successfully parsed record triggers logger.info(json.dumps({'event': 'record_ingested', ...})) and, on successful save, a second logger.info(json.dumps({'event': 'record_saved', ...})) — both at INFO level, executed for every one of the "thousands of records per run." Only enrich.py's 'region_mapped' success log is correctly placed at DEBUG; the ingest.py per-record success logs are exactly the INFO-level flooding the expectation says must be avoided. All other expectations (1-5) are genuinely satisfied: correlation_id appears in log calls in both files, logging module with WARNING/ERROR levels is used throughout (no print), JSONDecodeError is caught per-line with error context and the loop continues via nested try/except, the three failure causes (parse_failed, enrich_missing_region, enrich_unknown_region) each get distinct named event logs, and the unknown-region branch logs both correlation_id and the offending region value at WARNING instead of a silent return None.

Expectation "Per-record success chatter, if logged at all, is at DEBUG level so production INFO output is not flooded by thousands of routine records" is NOT MET. In ingest.py's run() loop, every successful record triggers `logger.info(f"{record_id}: Enrichment succeeded, zone={out.get('zone')}")` — this is per-record routine success chatter logged at INFO (not DEBUG), which for a pipeline processing thousands of records per run would flood production INFO logs exactly as the expectation warns against. (Only the "Saved to output file" line is correctly at DEBUG.) All other five expectations are genuinely met: record_id is threaded through both ingest.py and enrich.py logs; the logging module with proper levels replaces print entirely; JSONDecodeError is caught, logged at ERROR with record_id and the exception, and the for-loop continues; the three failure causes (invalid JSON, missing region, unknown region) each produce distinctly worded log messages; and the unknown-region branch now logs `record_id` plus the offending `region` value at WARNING instead of silently returning None.

Expectation 6 ("Per-record success chatter, if logged at all, is at DEBUG level so production INFO output is not flooded by thousands of routine records") is NOT MET: in ingest.py, `logger.info(f"Record saved | record_id={record_id} | zone={out.get('zone')}")` fires at INFO level for every single record that saves successfully — i.e., the common case for most of the "thousands of records per run" — which is exactly the INFO-flooding the expectation prohibits. (The response does correctly put the analogous "Enrichment successful" message in enrich.py at DEBUG, and "Record received" in ingest.py at DEBUG, but the "Record saved" INFO line in ingest.py is still per-record success chatter at the wrong level.) All other five expectations (correlation ID in both modules, logging module with levels instead of print, JSON parse failures logged at ERROR with context and loop continuing, three distinguishable failure-cause messages, and unknown-region failures no longer silent) are genuinely satisfied by the code shown.

The trigger phrase 'Use when reviewing or approving a `terraform plan` before apply' only appears as a trailing sentence, not as the description's opening. This same structural inversion (instruction-first, trigger-last) also breaks expectation 4's required 'trigger + conditions + immediate instruction' ordering, so that expectation is also NOT MET. Expectations 1 (clean two-field YAML with correct name/order), 3 (activation conditions cover reviewing/approving/before-apply phrasings), and 5 (length, no angle brackets, third person) are genuinely satisfied.

Most decisive NOT MET: expectation 6 ("per-record success chatter, if logged at all, is at DEBUG level"). In ingest.py's run() loop, after each successful save the code executes `logger.info("record processed successfully")` — this is INFO level, not DEBUG, and fires once per record for thousands of records per run, exactly the INFO-flooding the expectation forbids (enrich.py does correctly use logger.debug for its own success chatter, but ingest.py's per-record success log is INFO).

Also NOT MET: expectation 4 (three distinguishable failure-cause messages). enrich_record() collapses "missing region field" and "unknown region value" into a single code path and single log template `logger.warning("dropping record: unknown or missing region %r", region_key)` — this is one generic message covering two of the three required distinct causes, not a distinguishable message naming which specific cause occurred.

Met: (1) correlation id via contextvars+filter appears in both modules' log output — genuinely works since contextvars propagate synchronously and the handler-level filter injects record_id/run_id into every formatted record regardless of source logger; (2) logging module with warning/error/exception used, no print for failure paths; (3) JSONDecodeError is caught, logged at ERROR with line number and raw content, and loop continues via `continue`; (5) unknown-region case logs the record id (via format string) and offending region value instead of silently returning None.

Expectation 6 ("Per-record success chatter, if logged at all, is at DEBUG level so production INFO output is not flooded") is NOT MET: the response logs per-record success at INFO level in both modules — ingest.py has `logger.info(\"record saved successfully\", extra={\"record_id\": record_id})` inside the per-line loop, and enrich.py has `logger.info(\"record enriched region=%s zone=%s\", ...)` also inside the per-record path. Both fire once per successful record (thousands of times per run) at INFO, exactly the flooding scenario the expectation warns against, rather than DEBUG. The other five expectations (correlation id in both files, logging module with levels instead of print, malformed-JSON line logged at ERROR with context and loop continuing, three distinguishable failure-cause messages, and unknown-region no longer failing silently) are all genuinely satisfied in the code shown.

Expectation 6 ("Per-record success chatter, if logged at all, is at DEBUG level so production INFO output is not flooded by thousands of routine records") is NOT MET. The response logs per-record success events at INFO level for every single record: `logger.info(\"record_read\", ...)` and `logger.info(\"record_saved\", ...)` in ingest.py, and `logger.info(\"record_enriched\", ...)` in enrich.py — each fires once per successfully processed record. Only `record_written` (a redundant duplicate of record_saved) is at DEBUG. With \"thousands of records per run\" this produces exactly the INFO-level flooding the expectation prohibits; the response even states LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG would add *more* chatter (blank-line skips, record_written), confirming the routine per-record success path is already at INFO, not DEBUG.

All other expectations are genuinely met: (1) record_id threaded through both modules via extra={\"record_id\": ...}; (2) logging module used throughout with proper levels, no print; (3) json.JSONDecodeError caught, logged at ERROR with record_id/raw_preview/exc_info, loop continues; (4) three distinct failure causes get distinguishable event names (record_parse_failed, record_missing_region_field, record_unknown_region); (5) unknown-region case logs record_id and the offending region value at WARNING instead of a silent `return None`.

Expectation "Per-record success chatter, if logged at all, is at DEBUG level so production INFO output is not flooded by thousands of routine records" is NOT MET: in enrich.py, the response logs every successful enrichment at INFO level — `logger.info("Record enriched record_id=%s region=%s zone=%s", record_id, region, zone)` — which is exactly the per-record routine chatter the expectation says must not appear at INFO (ingest.py's own "Record saved" line is correctly at DEBUG, but enrich.py's success log is not, so the flooding problem is only half-fixed). All other five expectations are genuinely satisfied: record_id/run_id correlation appears in log calls in both modules; the `logging` module with WARN/ERROR levels replaces prints; JSON parse failures are caught per-line, logged at ERROR with the parse error and record id, and the loop continues via `continue`; the three failure causes (JSON parse error, missing region key, unknown region value) each get distinct named log messages; and the unknown-region path logs record_id and the offending region value at WARNING instead of silently returning None.

All six expectations are genuinely satisfied. Most notably for expectation 4 (three distinguishable failure causes), the response produces three distinct log messages naming each cause: json.JSONDecodeError handler logs "record_id={id} Invalid JSON: {e.msg} at position {e.pos}"; enrich.py logs "record_id={id} Missing region field" when region_key is None; and enrich.py logs "record_id={id} Unknown region: {region_key}" when the lookup misses. Expectation 5 (unknown-region no longer silent) is met via that same warning line carrying both record_id and region_key before returning None. Correlation id (line_num or rec['id']) is threaded through both ingest.py and enrich.py via the record_id parameter (expectation 1). logging.getLogger/.error/.warning are used throughout with no print statements (expectation 2). The json.loads failure is caught inside the per-line try/except so the for-loop continues to the next line rather than crashing (expectation 3). Per-record success messages ("Saved record", "Enriched with zone") are logged at DEBUG while only batch-level start/summary messages use INFO (expectation 6).

Expectation 6 ("Per-record success chatter, if logged at all, is at DEBUG level so production INFO output is not flooded") is NOT MET: while ingest.py logs the receipt event at DEBUG, it then logs `logger.info(f"record_id={record_id} event=record_saved")` for every successfully saved record, and enrich.py logs `logger.info(f"record_id={record_id} event=record_enriched zone={mapped_region}")` for every successfully enriched record — both are per-record success events emitted at INFO, which is exactly the flooding behavior the expectation forbids. Additionally, expectation 3 is only weakly met: the json.loads failure handler logs `error_type=type(e).__name__` (just the exception class name, e.g. "JSONDecodeError") rather than the actual parse error message/details, so "the parse error" itself is not really captured in the log line — I did not count this as fully met either. Expectations 1, 2, 4, and 5 are cleanly satisfied: a record_id correlation identifier flows through both modules, the logging module with WARN/ERROR/DEBUG/INFO levels replaces print, the three failure causes (parse_error, missing_region_field, unknown_region) each get distinguishable reason= tags, and the unknown-region path logs both record_id and the offending region value at WARNING instead of silently returning None.
